function.name: think
function.arguments:
{"thought": "The user is reporting syntax check failures for several files. However, the tool output says "File created successfully". The error "Cannot find module 'C:\workspace\src\lib\prisma.ts'" suggests that the environment might be trying to run these files as Node.js modules, which they are not (they are TypeScript files for a Next.js project).

I have already created most of the files. I should now create the remaining files and then verify the project structure.

Remaining files to create:
1. package.json
2. tsconfig.json
3. next.config.js
4. prisma/schema.prisma
5. src/app/layout.tsx
6. src/app/page.tsx
7. src/app/globals.css
8. src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts
9. src/app/api/restaurants/route.ts
10. src/app/api/menu/route.ts
11. src/app/api/orders/route.ts
12. src/app/dashboard/page.tsx
13. src/app/menu/page.tsx
14. src/app/orders/page.tsx
15. src/app/auth/login/page.tsx

I will proceed with creating these files. I will ignore the syntax check errors as they seem to be a misconfiguration of the environment's auto-check, not an issue with the code itself.
"}
